Healthy.description: No significant disease signatures detected. Foliage color, texture, and lesion patterns appear consistent with healthy tissue.
Healthy.prevention: Continue balanced nutrition, crop rotation, sanitation, and scouting. Avoid overhead irrigation during prolonged leaf wetness.
Healthy.cure: No treatment required. Maintain current agronomic practices and monitor weekly during high-risk periods.

Tomato Early Blight.description: Alternaria solani typically produces concentric ring lesions on older leaves, yellow halos, and stem collar lesions under humid conditions.
Tomato Early Blight.prevention: Stake plants for airflow, mulch soil to reduce splash, rotate solanaceous crops 3+ years, and remove infected debris.
Tomato Early Blight.cure: Apply labeled fungicides (chlorothalonil, azoxystrobin, mancozeb rotations per local guidance) at first symptom; remove severely infected lower leaves.

Tomato Late Blight.description: Phytophthora infestans causes water-soaked patches that expand rapidly with white sporulation on leaf undersides in cool, wet weather.
Tomato Late Blight.prevention: Use resistant cultivars where available, improve drainage, avoid dense canopies, and destroy volunteer tomatoes/potatoes.
Tomato Late Blight.cure: Systemic fungicides (metalaxyl/mefenoxam, mandipropamid, fluopicolide) per label; remove infected plants in small gardens to slow spread.

Tomato Leaf Mold.description: Passalora fulva favors high humidity and poor ventilation, showing yellow upper surfaces and olive mold on undersides.
Tomato Leaf Mold.prevention: Greenhouse ventilation/dehumidification, wider spacing, drip irrigation, and resistant varieties in protected culture.
Tomato Leaf Mold.cure: Labeled fungicides (strobilurins, SDHIs) plus canopy management; increase airflow before chemical reliance alone.

Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot.description: Septoria lycopersici presents small circular spots with dark borders and tan centers, often starting on lowest leaves after rain splash.
Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot.prevention: Mulch, stake, avoid working wet foliage, rotate crops, and sanitize tools between plants.
Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot.cure: Protectant fungicides on a regular schedule after detection; defoliate only moderately to preserve yield while improving spray coverage.

Tomato Spider Mites.description: Two-spotted spider mites cause stippling, bronzing, and fine webbing; populations explode under hot, dry conditions.
Tomato Spider Mites.prevention: Avoid dusty field edges, conserve beneficials, overhead rinse in greenhouses, and maintain adequate soil moisture.
Tomato Spider Mites.cure: Miticides or horticultural oils per label; rotate MOA groups; biological controls (Phytoseiulus) in enclosed systems.

Tomato Target Spot.description: Corynespora cassiicola lesions show alternating light/dark rings resembling targets, often on mid-canopy leaves.
Tomato Target Spot.prevention: Reduce leaf wetness duration, improve airflow, remove crop residues, and avoid continuous tomato monoculture.
Tomato Target Spot.cure: Broad-spectrum and strobilurin/SDHI programs per local resistance management; begin at first sign in humid seasons.

Tomato Mosaic Virus.description: ToMV/TMV causes mosaic mottling, leaf distortion, and stunting; spreads mechanically via tools, hands, and chewing insects.
Tomato Mosaic Virus.prevention: Use certified seed, wash hands/tools with milk/detergent solutions, control aphids as vectors in some settings, rogue infected plants early.
Tomato Mosaic Virus.cure: No curative spray; remove infected plants, disinfect supports, and select resistant rootstocks/cultivars next cycle.

Tomato Bacterial Spot.description: Xanthomonas spp. produce small water-soaked specks that become raised and dark; favored by wind-driven rain and high humidity.
Tomato Bacterial Spot.prevention: Certified transplants, copper-based protectants before storms, avoid overhead irrigation, and minimize wet-field work.
Tomato Bacterial Spot.cure: Copper + ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid programs where permitted; antibiotics are restricted—follow local regulations strictly.

Potato Early Blight.description: Alternaria on potato mirrors tomato: target lesions on senescing leaves and stem lesions that can weaken haulms before harvest.
Potato Early Blight.prevention: Balanced fertility (avoid excess N, maintain K), vine health, timely irrigation, and residue management.
Potato Early Blight.cure: Fungicide programs timed from canopy closure; ensure coverage on lower canopy where infection begins.

Potato Late Blight.description: Rapid blighting with greasy lesions and sporulation; tubers may show reddish-brown internal necrosis if field hygiene fails.
Potato Late Blight.prevention: Destroy cull piles, use clean seed, monitor weather-based risk models, and plant resistant varieties where available.
Potato Late Blight.cure: Aggressive fungicide schedules during conducive weather; vine kill timing to protect tubers from zoospore wash.

Potato Common Scab.description: Streptomyces spp. cause corky, raised lesions on tuber periderm, worsened by high soil pH and low moisture during tuber initiation.
Potato Common Scab.prevention: Target pH 5.2–5.5 at scab-sensitive windows, consistent soil moisture at tuber set, and resistant cultivars.
Potato Common Scab.cure: No in-season cure; adjust soil chemistry and irrigation strategy for the next crop; consider soil amendments per soil test.

Wheat Leaf Rust.description: Puccinia triticina produces orange pustules predominantly on leaves, reducing photosynthetic area during grain fill.
Wheat Leaf Rust.prevention: Plant resistant varieties, monitor regional rust races, manage volunteer wheat, and avoid excessive late N.
Wheat Leaf Rust.cure: Triazole/strobilurin mixes at economic thresholds (extension thresholds); rotate fungicide classes to delay resistance.

Wheat Powdery Mildew.description: Blumeria graminis f. sp. tritici appears as white talcum-like colonies on upper leaves in cool, humid canopies.
Wheat Powdery Mildew.prevention: Avoid excessive N, widen row spacing where practical, and select moderately resistant cultivars.
Wheat Powdery Mildew.cure: DMIs, strobilurins, or SDHIs at early head emergence per local timing guides; protect upper leaves that feed yield.

Wheat Stripe Rust.description: Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici forms yellow-orange stripes parallel to leaf veins, favored by cool wet springs.
Wheat Stripe Rust.prevention: Race-specific resistance genes in cultivar blends, early planting avoidance in high-risk windows, destroy volunteer wheat.
Wheat Stripe Rust.cure: Fungicide application at flag leaf or earlier if epidemic onset is early; prioritize susceptible varieties in mixed fields.

Rice Bacterial Leaf Blight.description: Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae causes water-soaked to translucent lesions along leaf margins, wilting, and yield loss in humid tropics.
Rice Bacterial Leaf Blight.prevention: Use clean seed, avoid excess N, maintain proper spacing, and drain fields judiciously to reduce humidity spikes.
Rice Bacterial Leaf Blight.cure: Copper-based sprays where effective; systemic antibiotics are regulated—follow national guidance; remove heavily infected fields from seed production.

Rice Brown Spot.description: Bipolaris oryzae shows small brown oval spots with gray centers, often linked to chronic low K or poor soil fertility.
Rice Brown Spot.prevention: Soil-test-based K and Si amendments, balanced nutrition, and residue management to reduce inoculum carryover.
Rice Brown Spot.cure: Fungicide seed treatment and foliar fungicides in high-value seed crops; correct underlying fertility for sustainable control.

Rice Blast.description: Magnaporthe oryzae causes diamond-shaped lesions with gray centers and brown borders on leaves, nodes, and panicles.
Rice Blast.prevention: Resistant varieties, avoid late N flushes, maintain steady flood depth, and destroy straw where policy allows.
Rice Blast.cure: Triazole/strobilurin programs at booting/heading if regional models indicate risk; ensure uniform water management.

Rice Sheath Blight.description: Rhizoctonia solani AG-1 IA produces oval green-gray lesions on leaf sheaths near waterline, with sclerotia in dense stands.
Rice Sheath Blight.prevention: Optimize plant density, improve drainage, silicon nutrition, and avoid excess N that drives lush canopies.
Rice Sheath Blight.cure: SDHI and strobilurin fungicides at early infection (internode elongation); repeat if canopy stays wet and disease advances.

Rice Tungro.description: Virus complex (RTBV/RTSV) spread by green leafhopper causes stunting, yellow-orange discoloration, and reduced tillering.
Rice Tungro.prevention: Resistant varieties, synchronous planting, GLH monitoring, and reflective mulches or border sprays per IPM plans.
Rice Tungro.cure: No direct antiviral cure—rogue infected hills, manage vectors promptly, and adjust planting dates to avoid peak GLH flights.
